title: Bakeridesia subcordata
intro: Bakeridesia subcordata, also known as Wild Salsify, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The health benefits of Bakeridesia subcordata include its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve digestion, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include inulin, a soluble fiber, along with sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Bakeridesia subcordata is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, as well as dried root extracts and capsules, which are used to support heart health and digestive function.
taxonomy: Bakeridesia subcordata belongs to the family Celastraceae. The genus Bakeridesia is a part of the larger clade of Celastrales. This plant is native to Central and South America and has been classified under the kingdom Plantae. More specifically, it falls under the division Magnoliophyta, which includes flowering plants. Its classification also includes the class Magnoliopsida, which includes dicotyledonous plants.
